audit_status.source_base: Greek Galatians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for heirs/guardians, elemental powers, Abba Spirit, Paul's concern, Hagar/Sarah allegory, freedom-child imagery. Anti-Jewish misuse warning included.

verses.restored: But I say, for as long a time as the heir is an infant, one differs nothing from an enslaved one, though being lord of all,
but is under guardians and household managers until the time appointed by the father.
So also we, when we were infants, were enslaved under the elemental powers/principles of the world.
But when the fullness of time came, the Divine sent out his Son, come from woman, come under Law,
so that he might redeem/release those under Law, so that we might receive adoption/placing-as-children.
And because you are sons/children, the Divine sent out the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
So you are no longer enslaved but son/child; and if son/child, also heir through the Divine.
But then, not knowing the Divine, you were enslaved to those by nature not gods.
But now, having known the Divine, or rather having been known by the Divine, how do you turn again to the weak and poor elemental powers/principles, to which you want to be enslaved again anew?
You observe days and months and seasons and years.
I fear for you, lest somehow I have labored into you in vain.
Become as I am, because I also became as you, siblings, I beg you. You wronged me in nothing.
But you know that through weakness of the flesh I announced good news to you formerly,
and your testing in my flesh you did not despise nor spit out, but received me as a messenger of the Divine, as Messiah Jesus.
Where then is your blessedness/flourishing? For I testify to you that, if possible, having dug out your eyes, you would have given them to me.
So then have I become your enemy by speaking truth to you?
They are zealous for you, not beautifully/well, but they want to shut you out, so that you may be zealous for them.
But it is beautiful/good to be zealous in a beautiful/good thing always, and not only when I am present with you.
My children, for whom I again labor in birth until Messiah is formed in you,
I wished to be present with you now and to change my voice, because I am perplexed about you.
Tell me, you who wish to be under Law, do you not hear the Law?
For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one from the enslaved woman and one from the free woman.
But the one from the enslaved woman was born according to flesh, and the one from the free woman through promise.
These things are being spoken allegorically; for these women are two covenants: one indeed from Mount Sinai bearing children into slavery, which is Hagar.
Now Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is enslaved with her children.
But the Jerusalem above is free, who is our mother.

For it is written: Rejoice, barren one who does not bear; break forth and cry aloud, you who do not labor in birth, because many are the children of the deserted one, more than of the one having the husband.
But you, siblings, according to Isaac, are children of promise.
But just as then the one born according to flesh pursued/persecuted the one according to Spirit, so also now.
But what does the Scripture say? Cast out the enslaved woman and her son, for the son of the enslaved woman will certainly not inherit with the son of the free woman.
Therefore, siblings, we are not children of an enslaved woman but of the free woman.

safety_note: Galatians 4's Hagar/Sarah allegory is Paul's sharp argument about covenantal freedom and Gentile inclusion; it must not be turned into contempt for Jewish people, Torah, Jerusalem, or enslaved women. The allegory is rhetorically volatile and should be handled with care.
